file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

input.new_string:
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domain names, subdomains,
# IP addresses, optional ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
url = Regex(
    r"https?://(?:(?:[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?\.)+[a-zA-Z]{2,}|(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3})(?::[0-9]{1,5})?(?:/[^\s]*)?"
)

# Matches 16-digit credit card numbers with no separators, or with
# space or hyphen separators grouped in fours (not mixed).
credit_card = Regex(r"[0-9]{4}([- ]?)[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}")

)

# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domain names, subdomains,
# IP addresses, optional ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
url = Regex(
    r"https?://(?:(?:[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?\.)+[a-zA-Z]{2,}|(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3})(?::[0-9]{1,5})?(?:/[^\s]*)?"
)

# Matches 16-digit credit card numbers with no separators, or with
# space or hyphen separators grouped in fours (not mixed).
credit_card = Regex(r"[0-9]{4}([- ]?)[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}")

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domain names, subdomains,
+# IP addresses, optional ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
+# Matches 16-digit credit card numbers with no separators, or with
+# space or hyphen separators grouped in fours (not mixed).

content.text: Now let's verify these work correctly with a quick test.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import re
from outlines import types

url_re = re.compile(types.url.pattern)
cc_re = re.compile(types.credit_card.pattern)

valid_urls = [
    'http://example.com',
    'https://example.com',
    'https://www.example.com',
    'https://sub.example.com',
    'http://a.io',
    'https://example.com:8080',
    'https://example.com/path/to/page',
    'https://example.com/path?query=1&other=2',
    'https://example.com/path#fragment',
    'http://192.168.1.1',
    'http://192.168.1.1:8080/path',
]
invalid_urls = [
    'ftp://example.com',
    'example.com',
    'http://',
    'http://example .com',
    'not a url at all',
]

print('--- URL ---')
for u in valid_urls:
    m = url_re.fullmatch(u)
    print('OK ' if m else 'FAIL', u)
for u in invalid_urls:
    m = url_re.fullmatch(u)
    print('OK ' if not m else 'FAIL', u)

valid_cc = ['1234567890123456', '1234-5678-9012-3456', '1234 5678 9012 3456']
invalid_cc = ['123456789012345', '1234-5678 9012-3456', '1234-56789012-3456']

print('--- Credit Card ---')
for c in valid_cc:
    m = cc_re.fullmatch(c)
    print('OK ' if m else 'FAIL', c)
for c in invalid_cc:
    m = cc_re.fullmatch(c)
    print('OK ' if not m else 'FAIL', c)
"

import re

url_re = re.compile(types.url.pattern)
cc_re = re.compile(types.credit_card.pattern)

valid_urls = [
    'http://example.com',

content.text: All test cases pass. Now let's verify Pydantic integration works as described in the requirements.

input.command:
python3 -c "
from outlines import types
from outlines.types.dsl import to_regex
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Model(BaseModel):
    site: types.url
    card: types.credit_card

print(Model.model_json_schema())
print(to_regex(types.url))
print(to_regex(types.credit_card))
print(types.url.matches('https://example.com/path'))
"

content.text: Everything integrates correctly with Pydantic and the DSL. Now let's write the patch file.

content.content:
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..36e3f3cd 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,13 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domain names, subdomains,
+# IP addresses, optional ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
+url = Regex(
+    r"https?://(?:(?:[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?\.)+[a-zA-Z]{2,}|(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3})(?::[0-9]{1,5})?(?:/[^\s]*)?"
+)
+
+# Matches 16-digit credit card numbers with no separators, or with
+# space or hyphen separators grouped in fours (not mixed).
+credit_card = Regex(r"[0-9]{4}([- ]?)[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}")

+# space or hyphen separators grouped in fours (not mixed).
+credit_card = Regex(r"[0-9]{4}([- ]?)[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}\1[0-9]{4}")

content.text: Added `url` and `credit_card` regex types to `outlines/types/__init__.py`, matching the pattern used by existing types like `email` and `isbn`. Both integrate with `Regex`/`to_regex()`, Pydantic model JSON schema generation, and `.matches()`/`.validate()`. Verified with manual tests covering valid/invalid URLs (domains, subdomains, IPs, ports, paths, query strings, fragments, bad protocols, spaces) and credit card formats (no separator, consistent space/hyphen separators, rejecting wrong digit counts and mixed separators). Diff written to `patch.txt`.
